title: Interlynk Component Manifest v1
description: Hand-curated component manifest consumed by `sbomasm generate sbom`. See docs/spec/generate-sbom.md for the full specification, worked examples, and field semantics.

schema.description: Schema marker. Must be the literal string 'interlynk/component-manifest/v1'. Files without this field are silently skipped by `sbomasm generate sbom --recurse`.

components.description: List of third-party components. Required; may be empty but then the manifest contributes nothing to the merged SBOM.

type.description: Component type. Mirrors CycloneDX component type. Defaults to 'library' if omitted.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the component is and why it is in the project.

purl.description: Package URL. For patched/vendored components, MUST NOT equal any pedigree.ancestors[].purl — see 'Purl on patched components' in the spec.

external_references.description: External references (website, VCS, distribution, etc.). Distribution URL is strongly recommended for vendored and native components.

hashes.description: Component hashes. Each entry is one of three forms: literal value, single-file reference, or directory reference with optional extension filter. See 'File-based hashes' in the spec.

scope.description: required — ships in runtime artifact AND is reachable at runtime. optional — ships in runtime artifact but may not be reached (feature flags, plugins, platform-conditional). excluded — does NOT ship in runtime artifact (build-time only: compilers, test harnesses, header-only dev deps). Rule of thumb: if the component's bytes are inside the final artifact a user downloads, it's 'required' or 'optional'; otherwise 'excluded'.

depends-on.description: References to components this depends on, in name@version or full purl format. If omitted, the component is a top-level dependency of the primary application from .artifact-metadata.yaml.

tags.description: String tags for per-build filtering via `sbomasm generate sbom --tags` / `--exclude-tags`. Can carry platform names (linux, macos, ios) for platform-specific SBOMs.

oneOf.description: SPDX license ID or expression (e.g. 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0 OR MIT'). Shorthand for { id: '...' }.

text.description: Inline license text. Use when the license text must be embedded directly in the manifest.

file.description: Relative path (from the manifest file) to a file containing the full license text. Read at generate time. Recommended for vendored code — commit the upstream LICENSE file next to the vendored source.

license.description: Four equivalent forms: 'MIT' | { id: 'MIT' } | { id: 'MIT', text: '...' } | { id: 'MIT', file: './LICENSE' }. Pick the richest form available. License expressions are passed through unparsed.

type.description: Reference type. Mirrors CycloneDX externalReference types.

algorithm.description: Hash algorithm. Literal `value` entries accept any CycloneDX-supported algorithm and are passed through unchanged. The file/path forms only compute SHA-256 and SHA-512 — other algorithms in those forms are a generate-time error.

value.description: Literal hex digest. Pasted by hand, frozen. Use this form when you have a pre-computed upstream digest that must not drift.

file.description: Relative path (from the manifest) to a single regular file whose hash is computed at generate time. Pointing at a directory is a hard error — use `path` for that.

path.description: Relative path (from the manifest) to a file or directory. When pointing at a directory, the generator walks recursively, filters by `extensions` (if given), skips symlinks, excludes hidden files and directories (dotfile basenames), and combines per-file digests via a sorted-manifest scheme (git-tree-object style) for determinism across filesystems. No-match is a hard error.

extensions.description: Optional file-extension filter for the directory form of `path`. Accepts entries like 'c', '.c', or '*.c' (leading dot and leading '*.' are both tolerated, case-insensitive). When omitted, every regular non-hidden file under the directory is included.

hash.description: Three mutually-exclusive forms: literal { algorithm, value }, single file { algorithm, file }, or directory { algorithm, path, extensions? }. Only one of value/file/path may be set per entry.

pedigree.description: Vendored/patched code provenance. Mirrors the CycloneDX pedigree model. If a component has a pedigree block with ancestors, its own top-level purl MUST NOT equal any ancestor purl — the generator hard-errors on that collision even without --strict.

ancestors.description: Upstream components this was forked from. A component's top-level purl MUST NOT equal any ancestor purl.

notes.description: Free-form notes about the fork history, local modifications, or reasoning.

type.description: Patch type. Mirrors CycloneDX patch type.

diff.description: Patch contents. Either inline `text` or a `url` pointing at a local patch file or remote URL. Local relative paths are read at generate time and inlined as text.

resolves.description: Issues this patch resolves (e.g. CVEs, bug trackers).
